description_long: This is an irregularly shaped rock with a roughly triangular profile and a coarse, granular surface. Its coloration is predominantly a mottled mix of gray, olive, and tan, with one face showing a lighter, almost white patch that appears freshly broken or less weathered. The rock's texture is rugged and uneven, with visible mineral grains and a generally matte finish. Edges are jagged and not smoothed, suggesting it has not been significantly weathered or transported far from its source. The mass and density suggest it is composed primarily of granite or a similar igneous rock. This object would be a substantial obstacle if encountered in an urban environment, such as a park or roadside.
description: A coarse, irregularly shaped rock with mottled gray and olive tones, featuring a lighter broken face and a rugged, granular texture.
description_view_0: This view highlights the lighter, broken face of the rock and shows its angular, jagged edges.
description_view_1: The rock appears darker and more uniform in this view, with the rough, granular surface texture most prominent.
description_view_2: This angle shows the broadest face of the rock, with mottled gray and olive coloration and a slightly convex surface.
description_view_3: The lighter, broken section is visible on the lower right, with the rest of the rock appearing darker and more weathered.
